name: Manta Point
description: This dive site off Nosy Be in Madagascar’s Diana region offers thrilling encounters with graceful manta rays gliding through the Mozambique Channel. The site features a cleaning station where mantas hover near coral formations, allowing close observation. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, attracting pelagic life like reef sharks and occasional whale sharks. The sandy bottom slopes to deeper waters, with visibility often exceeding 20 meters. Best dived during the dry season when mantas are most active. Suitable for intermediate divers due to occasional strong currents.

name: Arches
description: This site off Nosy Be features stunning underwater arches covered in soft corals and sponges. Strong currents bring pelagics like reef sharks and schooling fish. The arches create swim-throughs at various depths, with vibrant marine life in every crevice. Visibility is often excellent, making it ideal for photographers. Best suited for experienced divers due to the currents.

name: Pain De Sucre
description: A stunning dive site off Madagascar's northwest coast, featuring dramatic rock formations and vibrant coral gardens. The underwater landscape is rich with reef fish, moray eels, and occasional reef sharks. Clear waters offer excellent visibility, while gentle currents make it suitable for divers of various experience. The site's unique topography includes swim-throughs and overhangs teeming with marine life. Expect warm water temperatures year-round, ideal for extended bottom times. The area remains relatively uncrowded, providing a serene diving experience.

name: Roches A Gaby
description: Tucked off Nosy Iranja, this site features dramatic granite boulders creating swim-throughs and overhangs. Healthy soft corals and sponges cover the rocks, attracting reef fish like anthias and butterflyfish. Occasional reef sharks and turtles pass through. Currents can be strong, making it ideal for drift diving. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters. Best dived at slack tide when conditions ease.

name: Les Tetons
description: This dive site off Nosy Mitsio features dramatic underwater pinnacles rising from the seabed, covered in vibrant soft corals. Expect strong currents that attract schooling fish, reef sharks, and occasional manta rays. The clear waters offer excellent conditions for drift diving along the steep walls. The area is known for its rich biodiversity, including turtles and colorful reef fish. Depth ranges from 10 to 30 meters, suitable for intermediate to advanced divers. The remote location ensures pristine conditions with minimal human impact.

name: Epave Mitsio
description: This wreck dive off Nosy Be lies in the Mozambique Channel, resting at 25 meters. The intact hull attracts schools of snapper, batfish, and barracuda. Coral encrusts the structure while moray eels peer from crevices. Strong currents bring pelagics like tuna and reef sharks. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters. The wreck's depth makes it ideal for advanced divers. Nearby reefs teem with colorful anthias and anemones. Night dives reveal octopus and lobster activity. The site offers both wreck exploration and vibrant marine life encounters.

name: Cathedrale
description: A stunning dive site off Madagascar's coast, known for its dramatic limestone formations resembling cathedral arches. The clear waters of the Mozambique Channel reveal vibrant coral garden walls teeming with reef fish, moray eels, and occasional reef sharks. Depths range from 10 to 30 meters, offering both drift and relaxed exploration. Strong currents sometimes sweep through the arches, attracting pelagic visitors. The site's unique topography creates swim-throughs and caverns, perfect for adventurous divers. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters, showcasing the thriving marine ecosystem. Best dived during Madagascar's dry season for calmer conditions.
